creative_brief: Clean, farm-focused design with a hero image of cattle on pasture at Rachel's Farm. Earthy color palette with plenty of white space to let the story breathe.
subject: Tuesday morning at Rachel's Farm
preheader: What I saw changed how I think about quality beef forever
body:
Tuesday, 6:47am. I'm standing in a paddock at Rachel's Farm in Kempsey, watching something you'll never see at a supermarket.

Rachel Ward — yes, the actor turned regenerative farming advocate — is checking on her cattle. These aren't just any cows. They're 100% grass-fed, roaming freely on pasture, building soil health with every step.

No hormones. No antibiotics. No feedlot finishing. Just cattle doing what cattle do best — turning grass into the kind of beef that reminds you what quality actually tastes like.

Here's what hit me: while supermarket beef travels 2,400km and sits in cold storage for 14-21 days, Rachel's beef goes straight from her farm to our hub to your kitchen. Days old, not weeks old.

That's the FarmThru difference. We'll show you where your food comes from — because when you know the farm, you taste the difference.

Rachel's regenerative beef is just one reason we're building something completely different. A grocery store that puts craft and quality first, with a hub-and-collect model that keeps costs down for everyday families.

Want to be part of this food revolution?
